name: Ukrainian SSR (1937–1949)

colors.symbolism: the revolutionary struggle of the working class
the hammer, sickle and Cyrillic abbreviation

description: A red field with the gold hammer-and-sickle above the gold Cyrillic letters У.Р.С.Р. (UkrSSR), both placed in the upper hoist.
history: Adopted in 1937, this design added a gold hammer-and-sickle (matching the all-Union Soviet flag) above the Ukrainian-language acronym У.Р.С.Р. (Ukrainska Radianska Sotsialistychna Respublika), bringing Ukraine's flag into broader Soviet visual conformity. Replaced in 1949 by the red-and-light-blue design with hammer-and-sickle-and-star.

features.symbolism: the alliance of industrial workers and the peasantry under Soviet socialism

features.role: Cyrillic letters У.Р.С.Р. (Ukrainian-language acronym for the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic)
